TextENGNew: ...There was no way I could have slept.
It was only after the line went dead that I realized she was the one person who truly understood and shared in my predicament.
I waited desperately for Shion to cool off and call me back.
What if the phone rang while I was fast asleep...?
But no matter how long I waited, it never rang.
...Exhausted, each time my consciousness began to fade, I would jolt upright, convinced I had heard the phone ring.
In the end, I brought the cordless phone into my futon and fell asleep clutching it.
...Not that it solved anything...
Morning came, and by then I was certain she would not be calling back.
...That was precisely when a crushing wave of drowsiness finally came crashing down on me.
``Damn it...!@k@vS02/01/130100429. Get a grip, Keiichi Maebara! This is no time to be dozing off!``
I slapped myself across the face. Hard. Again.
I went to the washroom and washed my face -- something I never usually bothered with.
...That still wasn't enough to wake me up, so I even brushed my teeth.
Only after all that did I finally feel alert.
Mom noticed my bizarre behavior and stared at me wide-eyed...
``What's the matter, Keiichi?@k@vS02/00/132700021. Are you going somewhere today?``
``Keiichi-kun, you don't look so good today either.@k@vS02/02/130200101. ...Do you think you might have a cold?``
``...It's not a cold, just sleep deprivation.@k@vS02/01/130100433. If I start spacing out and saying weird stuff, do me a favor and cover for me...``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS02/02/130200103. I wonder what kind of things you'd say if you were half-asleep! I wonder!@k@vS02/02/130200104. ...I'm kind of looking forward to it, actually. Hau~``
I had been utterly beaten down and still hadn't recovered this morning. That was precisely why Rena's guileless smile was such a blessing.
``Huh?@k@vS02/02/130200106. Mii-chan, you look like you didn't get much sleep either! Don't you?``
...Mion had gone home early yesterday due to lack of sleep, and she still didn't look well this morning.
Her usual bright energy had completely vanished.
``...Yeah.@k@vS02/03/130300143. Didn't get to bed until around three.@k@vS02/03/130300144. ...I'm so tired.``

``Th-Three?! Two nights in a row?!@k@vS02/01/130100435. Come on... there's a limit to how late you can stay up, you know?!@k@vS02/01/130100436. I bet you got hooked on a manga series and ended up reading it from volume one all the way through.@k@vS02/01/130100437. Yeah, yeah. I've done that myself.``
I was trying to lighten the mood with a joke -- an attempt at being considerate.
...But neither Mion nor Rena so much as cracked a smile, let alone played along.
Rena's expression darkened in an instant.
``...............Huh... could it be... about the village chief?@k@vS02/02/130200108. He still hasn't been found?``
``Hey, hold on a second.@k@vS02/01/130100439. What do you mean the village chief hasn't been found? What's going on?``
Even the classroom, normally overflowing with cheerful, playful voices, was filled with a different kind of murmur today.
``Good morning, Keiichi-san.``
``...Have you heard?@k@vS02/04/130400038. It seems like something terrible has happened.``
``...I don't really know this village chief guy, but... he's old, right?@k@vS02/01/130100441. Couldn't he just be wandering around in a senile daze?``
``The village chief is absolutely not senile.@k@vS02/04/130400040. He teaches calligraphy and kendo -- he's a very vigorous man.``
``......He's probably just lost somewhere, like me.``
``...A l-lost Rika-chan!@k@vS02/02/130200110. Hau~~~! I wanna take you home~~!!``
...Getting lost is only adorable when kids like Rika-chan do it.@k ...Some worn-out old man getting lost doesn't exactly inspire moe!
...While I was entertaining dumb thoughts like that, our teacher arrived.
Everyone scrambled to their seats.
``Everyone.@k@vS02/22/131400009. Good morning.``
Good morning!
The usual chorus.@k ...But the mood was heavy.

``Some of you may have already heard this, but...@k@vS02/22/131400011. ...last night, even as it grew very late, the village chief never returned home.@k@vS02/22/131400012. Did anyone here happen to see the village chief yesterday?``
...In the silence, one or two hands went up, and they described when and where they had seen him.
...None of it seemed to be anything crucial, however, and everyone began whispering amongst themselves...
``If any of you see the village chief, please let an adult know right away.@k@vS02/22/131400014. ...Now then, let's begin today's lessons.``
And with that, the usual lessons began.
The hushed rumors bloomed anew at lunchtime, spreading like flowers across every corner.
...Snatches of various conversations reached my ears.
Nearly all of them were about the village chief's disappearance.
...Piecing everything together, the story went something like this.
Yesterday evening, the village chief went to a meeting at the shrine's assembly hall.
The meeting ended after nightfall, and everyone dispersed.
The village chief's house was a bit removed from the center of Hinamizawa.
...It was already late, and Hinamizawa has almost no streetlights.@k ...No one saw the village chief on his way home.
But it would have been dinnertime, so he would not have taken any detours -- he should have headed straight home.
...And yet, no matter how long his family waited...@k ...he never came back.
An accident was one possibility.
...Considering every scenario, they searched the irrigation ditches, the wells, even the muddy rice paddies, but found nothing.
Of course... there were limits to what a search in the dead of night could accomplish.

The police planned to wait for daylight and then conduct a joint search with the village's young men's association.@k If he still wasn't found after that, they would apparently organize a sweep of the mountains.
Even now, as we sat here, the adults were no doubt searching for him...
...The longer he went unfound...
...the more time that passed...
...the more one thought reared its ugly head... ...the curse.@k The whispers of Onikakushi -- the 'spiriting away by demons.'
...Had he become this year's victim of Oyashiro-sama's curse, which had been occurring every year without fail...?
That terrifying possibility, which everyone thought about but no one dared speak aloud, was spreading through the village like a slow poison...
Had he been spirited away by demons... offered up as a sacrifice to appease Oyashiro-sama's wrath...?
My classmates whispered to each other that the curse hadn't happened this year, so there shouldn't need to be any sacrifices.
...But I... I knew better.
I knew the curse had struck again this year, and that two people had already died.
...I don't mean to sound callous, but... I hadn't expected the village chief to be the one who vanished.
The reason was simple.
...Shion and I should have been higher on the list.
We were four intruders who had trampled through the sacred ritual storehouse, breaking every taboo.
If Tomitake-san and Takano-san had already met mysterious deaths... then naturally, Shion and I should have been next......
``...This time, it should've been my turn... or Shion's... right?``
...I let out a bitter laugh.

I had screamed at Shion over the phone that I didn't want to meet such a terrible fate, and yet...@k Now that someone else had disappeared, my reaction was 'if anyone was going to vanish, it should have been me first.' How absurd.
......Onigafuchi -- the bottomless swamp where, according to legend, they once drowned their sacrifices.
...If the thought had occurred to me, the villagers must have suspected the same place.
Were police divers dredging the bottom of that swamp even now...?
...They wouldn't find anything.
...Not a single one of the past missing persons had ever been found.
Onigafuchi had always been the prime suspect, but never once had a body been recovered from its depths.
...It was a cursed, bottomless swamp -- once you sank in, you never came back...
...I could hear someone in the classroom telling their friend exactly that...
``...Come to think of it, Satoshi-san went missing around this time last year too, didn't he?``
Huh?@k Satoshi?
......That name rang a bell.@k ...He was... right, the one who disappeared in last year's curse.
The conversation was happening at a nearby desk, so I could hear every word clearly.
``...Didn't we search the whole village like this for Satoshi-san too?``
``We never found him either.@k@vS02/00/boyA03003. ...What was the deal with that again?@k@vS02/00/boyA03004. ...It wasn't a demoning away, right?``
``Right.@k@vS02/00/boyaB03003. I heard he withdrew all his savings and ran away from home.@k@vS02/00/boyaB03004. The police said someone saw him boarding the bullet train at Nagoya Station.``
.........Ran away from home, huh.
I wasn't particularly interested in the plausible cover stories.
Every past disappearance had its own convenient explanation.
The culprit was on the run, or the body simply hadn't been found yet.
...None of them had simply vanished into thin air without some kind of rationale.
But still... they vanished.
...Every time the curse claimed a life, someone else disappeared alongside it, as if by clockwork.
However plausible the individual reasons might have been, they still disappeared.
.........In that sense, this time might be the first genuine demoning away.
The village chief's disappearance was the first such case with no plausible reason behind it at all.
...Of course, even noticing a trivial detail like that probably wouldn't help solve anything...
``...It looks like I messed up the seasoning today.@k@vS02/02/130200112. Ehehe... sorry about that.``
Rena's bento, which would normally have been devoured first, sat there completely untouched.
...Not because Rena's cooking tasted bad or anything...
``N-No, that's not it at all!@k@vS02/04/VT1_satoko2002. Your lunch is just as delicious as always!``
Satoko reached her chopsticks into Rena's bento and began shoveling side dishes into her mouth one after another.
``Yes, so delicious!@k@vS02/04/130400044. Truly exquisite today!@k@vS02/04/130400045. Nom nom!!``
``Ahahaha... hey, you know those are pumpkin croquettes, right?@k@vS02/02/130200114. Are you okay with that?``
``If it's your pumpkin, Rena-san, then it's absolutely delicious!``
changeReason: Character name 'Rena' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ...Did Satoko hate pumpkin?
Even so, she forced a smile and kept wolfing it down with gusto.
...Everyone understood.
Nobody's lunch was going to taste good today.
...And yet there was Satoko, stuffing her cheeks and insisting over and over how wonderful it was.
``...If it's that good, I'd better grab some too. At this rate, Satoko's going to eat everything.``
``I-It's just so tasty, I really think Keiichi-san should have some too...!``
``Every now and then I've gotta show what a generous upperclassman I am.@k@vS02/01/130100445. All right, don't hold back!@k@vS02/01/130100446. Today you can have my share too!``
``Fuh... fuwaaaahhh...!!``
...She truly despised pumpkin from the bottom of her heart.
And that was precisely what made her clumsy kindness so touching.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ......I grabbed the top of her head and ruffled her hair affectionately.
...Satoko was crying with almost theatrical intensity, tears spilling freely down her face.

``Just kidding.@k@vS02/01/130100448. ...I'm not mature enough to give away food that good.@k@vS02/01/130100449. Rena, you eat too.@k@vS02/01/130100450. Mine's just leftover oden from last night, but oden always tastes better the next day.``
``...Yeah!@k@vS02/02/130200116. Oh, the daikon has turned such a beautiful, translucent color -- it looks delicious.@k@vS02/02/130200117. Well then, I think I'll help myself~``
Rena reached out with her chopsticks, ...and at last, the familiar lunchtime warmth began to return.
But only at our little group of desks.
...The grim talk of Oyashiro-sama's curse, now five years running, showed no sign of stopping.@k The entire classroom remained shrouded in a somber atmosphere...
...Oyashiro-sama's curse had struck for five consecutive years.
Everyone had desperately wanted last year to be the end of it.
...What was Oyashiro-sama's curse, really?
They were going to build a dam in Hinamizawa.
The people behind it were the enemy.
So they were cursed.
...I could accept that much.
...But the dam project had been frozen years ago.@k So why did the curse keep going, so relentlessly...?
I caught myself and gave my cheeks a few light slaps.
Everyone was trying so hard to brighten things up, and here I was, the first one to spiral back into talk of the curse...!
I forced the dark thoughts out of my head.
...Banishing the curse of Oyashiro-sama from my mind was actually quite convenient.
...Because it also meant banishing the most terrifying thought of all -- that I myself could be the curse's next target...
``...Mion's sure taking her time, isn't she?@k@vS02/01/130100452. How long can a bathroom break possibly take?``
Mion had said she was going to the bathroom and left, but she still hadn't come back.
``...Ahahaha.@k@vS02/02/130200119. She's probably napping somewhere.``
``...She was probably up late last night helping search for the village chief.``
...I managed a rueful smile and let out a sigh.
...Today was no good.
No matter how hard we tried to lighten things up, the conversation always circled back to the village chief.@k ...It was just that kind of day.
...I had only just transferred here, so I could barely imagine it, but...the accumulated shock of a curse persisting for five straight years must have been enormous.
......Perhaps I needed to understand that.

``...When Mii-chan was little, the village chief used to dote on her so much.@k@vS02/02/130200122. Even when she was at her most mischievous... he adored her.@k@vS02/02/130200123. ...She's told me about it many times.``
......I see.@k ...Mion was hurting just as much.
Having someone vanish because of a curse was terrifying in itself.
...But if that person was someone close to you... the shock must be many times worse.
Satoko seemed to sense this too; she bit her lower lip and cast her eyes downward...
``Oh, come to think of it, Rika-chan's been gone a while too.@k@vS02/01/130100454. Maybe she and Mion are having a nice little nap together?``
changeReason: Character name 'Mion' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ``...Rika wasn't out that late.@k@vS02/04/130400051. ...The moment Rika yawned, everyone started fussing over her -- 'Good night, good night, dear!'``
Now that she mentioned it, during the festival there had been plenty of old folks rubbing their prayer beads and practically worshipping Rika-chan...
I could easily picture the elderly fawning over her.
......Had Rika-chan mastered the art of getting doted on...???
``...Rika-chan really is the idol of the elderly, isn't she?@k@vS02/01/130100456. Is she their vision of the perfect granddaughter or something?``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS02/02/130200125. I have no idea about that~!@k@vS02/02/130200126. B-B-But I want to see Rika-chan yawn~~~!!``
``...I bet it's all like, fwaahh~...``
``...Hau~~~!! I wanna take her home~~~!!!``
``......I don't personally think it's that cute.@k@vS02/04/130400053. ...It's more of a 'gwaaah,' mouth gaping wide enough to see the back of her throat...?``
``R-R-R-Rika-chan's enormous yawn~~~!! Hau~~~~!!!!``
At times like these, Rena was invaluable...@k At last, I was able to laugh from the bottom of my heart.
...Oh, right.
I'm on day duty today.@k I need to go water the flower beds.
The flower beds are right in front of the staff room, so they'd notice immediately if I slacked off.
``I-I-I think Satoko-chan is just as kyute, not just Rika-chan~!!@k@vS02/02/130200131. Huuuuge~~yaaaawn~~!!!``
``Gh-Gh-Gh-Gheeehhhh~~~...!!@k@vS02/04/130400055. C-Can't... breathe......``
``H-Hau~~!!@k@vS02/02/130200133. I saw it! I caught a glimpse of something kyute!!@k@vS02/02/130200134. Satoko-chan's yawn is kyute too! I'm taking you home~~!!!!``
...Rena had Satoko in a chokehold. I don't think that qualifies as a yawn anymore...
...Rena is... ...I can't say it out loud, but... she's a certified... how do I put this... (sweat)
The strangled-chicken noises coming out of Satoko were so absurd I couldn't help but laugh.
Before I knew it, the classroom was filled with laughter at their antics.
...Maybe Rena was doing it on purpose.
Even if she wasn't, this raucous nonsense was a godsend right now.
Anyway.
...Let me go water the flowers while I still have the time.
Careful not to disturb the room's newfound peace, I slipped out of the classroom as quietly as I could.
The watering can was hanging out to dry beside the kitchenette.
This building had originally been an all-male forestry service office, so apparently it never had anything so refined as a flower bed.
When it was decided that the school would share the building, the garden was created as a small token of gratitude.
Now, about these flower beds... they were surprisingly... extensive.
They wound all the way around.
They were everywhere.
There were so many that I'd need to refill the watering can five or six times to cover them all.
The kid who was on day duty with me was supposed to help, but...@k she was a girl in the youngest grade, a full size smaller than even Rika-chan.
I couldn't very well make a kid who struggled just to lift a full watering can help with this...
......If I'd been paired with someone who actually enjoyed this sort of thing, they'd handle it all on their own and I could relax.
``Complaining won't get it done.@k@vS02/01/130100458. ...Might as well give up and get to work.``
Watering can in hand, I was about to head out through the entrance when one of the forestry service workers called out to me.
``Hey kid, there's a vegetable garden behind the warehouse, right?@k@vS02/00/manA03002. It's looking pretty wilted.@k@vS02/00/manA03003. Give that a watering too, would you?``
``Oh, you mean the curry garden?@k@vS02/01/130100460. The one that only grows potatoes and carrots and other curry ingredients?``
That was definitely our curry-obsessed teacher's personal vegetable garden...
Making students maintain it was a blatant abuse of authority, if you asked me...
But I couldn't say that to his face, so I resigned myself to fulfilling my day duties.
The sunlight outside was harsh.
...The grand chorus of cicadas was asserting, with full force, that today was no different from any other day.
As I passed by the classroom... somehow, Rena and Satoko had escalated into an all-out brawl.
While I was gone, the desks had been arranged in a circle like ringside seats, and the whole class was cheering them on.
......Yeah. This was how things should be.
Once school let out, we'd have to return to reality -- where talk of the village chief's disappearance would be everywhere again.
At the very least, while we were at school, I wanted everyone to forget it all and be as lively as they had been yesterday.
With that in mind, even Satoko's vicious elbow strikes and Rena's flying reverse roundhouse kicks were a heartwarming sight.
...Even if the collateral damage flipped my desk over...@k Agh, my pencil case--!!@k ...Ngh...... I'll let it slide today.
Driven by the sole desire to finish quickly, I worked through the watering with crisp efficiency.
Done!@k ...Or so I thought, right until I remembered the teacher's curry garden still needed watering.
``......What a pain... but something tells me I'll pay for it later if I don't.``
Giving up, I refilled the can and headed behind the warehouse.
It was always damp back here, with rumors of slugs and pill bugs lurking about -- one of those places students tended to avoid.
So when I ran into someone there, I was genuinely startled.
``Whoa!!@k@vS02/01/130100463. Oh... Rika-chan.@k@vS02/01/130100464. ...Don't scare me like that...``
Rika-chan was just standing there, utterly lifeless... like a doll, staring blankly into space...
...I was the only one startled.
Rika-chan didn't even seem to register my presence.
...At last, she noticed me.
...Her expression was clearly wrong.
...The skin around her eyes was bright red... streaked with tears and smudged with dirt.
...There were blades of grass tangled in her hair, and her clothes were soiled as though she had fallen.
``Rika-chan...?!@k@vS02/01/130100467. Did you... did you hurt yourself?@k@vS02/01/130100468. Did you fall...?!``
``......*hic*... N-No, that's not it.@k@vS02/05/130500029. ...That's not it.``
Rika-chan said, wiping her eyes, forcing her voice to sound as bright as she could manage...
But no matter how you looked at her, nothing about her was normal.
``What happened?@k@vS02/01/130100470. Don't tell me you were napping on the warehouse roof and... fell off...?!``
``......Don't worry about me.@k@vS02/05/130500031. There's something I need to ask you, Keiichi.``
...Rika-chan came over and wrapped her arms around my waist.
At first, I thought she was just being playful.
...But soon I realized... she was making sure I couldn't escape the question she was about to ask...
A chill ran through me.
``Wh-What... ...what do you want to ask me?``
She peered up into my eyes.
She gazed up at me... staring intently, unflinchingly.
......It was as though her eyes were trying to extract the answer from mine without even needing to ask the question.
That terrified me... and I looked away from her gaze...
``......Keiichi.@k@vS02/05/130500033. On the night of the festival... did you do something bad...?``
My entire body jolted as if struck at my very core.
...Rika-chan, clinging to me as she was, must have felt that tremor.
A chill crawled upward from my feet... spreading across my back and freezing me from head to toe... before finally reaching my brain...
``......Keiichi.@k@vS02/05/130500035. Are you sure you don't remember anything?``
My head... went numb, as if wrapped in fog...@k ...I couldn't... make sense of anything anymore.
Mion asked me.@k Ooishi-san asked me.@k Shion asked me.@k ......And now Rika-chan was asking me......
We thought what happened that night was a secret... that nobody knew... but in truth... everyone knew...?!
They all knew... and one by one, they came to ask me.
Again and again... until I finally admitted it.
...When I thought about it... it was painfully obvious that people knew.
...After all... weren't Tomitake-san and Takano-san's deaths proof enough?
...How... how should I answer?
Entering the forbidden ritual storehouse was wrong... and even though I regretted it this deeply, was she saying it still wasn't enough...?
...But... the words that would come out of my mouth were, for the most part, already decided.
``I-I... I'm not exactly a well-behaved kid.@k@vS02/01/130100473. ...I've done so many bad things, I couldn't even tell you which one you mean.``
Just as I had with everyone else...... I deflected.
Rika-chan... gazed up at me with those innocent eyes of hers.
...Her gaze was so piercing it hurt, and I had to look away.
After a prolonged mutual silence, Rika-chan released me.
``...I'm sorry for asking something so strange.@k@vS02/05/130500039. Please forget about it...``
she said with a smile -- her usual, gentle smile.
...Then, patter patter patter... she scurried off as if nothing had happened.
......Her retreating figure seemed to silently accuse me...
If my voice didn't reach her, I was going to let it go.
...But it did reach her.
...Rika-chan spun around and waited for what I had to say next.
......It was my turn to have questions.
But... asking them... might as well be the same as admitting everything...
``...Hey... Rika-chan.``
...If everyone already knows... then surely I can at least... ask this much... right?
Why does everyone keep asking me about the night of the festival?
...That night... had I really done something... so terribly wrong...?
I was deeply sorry about it... and besides, I only looked...
I didn't break anything, and I didn't take anything.@k ...Really!
...But still... Tomitake-san... and Takano-san... died in such horrible ways.
...Given what happened to those two... there was obviously no reason 'it' would spare me alone...
Rika-chan tilted her head with an endearing little gesture... and waited for my words.
......But... if I were to admit what happened that night... she might do a complete about-face, as if flipping a switch.
...That's right.
...Rika-chan... was the shrine maiden...
Wouldn't she be the very last person to forgive someone for breaking a taboo...?
When I thought about it, confessing to her... would be far more terrifying than telling Mion or Ooishi-san, wouldn't it...?
...Rika-chan could have given up on me and walked away.
...But she didn't.
......She simply, simply... quietly, quietly...... waited for me to say it myself.
...Say it, Keiichi Maebara.
...If you truly feel remorse... then you should say so, and apologize.
True enough... Takano-san and the others' gruesome deaths gave me more than enough reason to hesitate.
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ........The grinding machine called 'inner conflict' was crushing my brain to pulp......
...The juice squeezed out of it... turned to sweat pouring from every pore... dripping away in heavy drops...
Rika-chan stood on her tiptoes... trying to reach up and pat me on the head.
``......I don't really understand what's troubling you, Keiichi.``
...I had no words.@k ......I lowered my head slightly, and Rika-chan's small palm was able to reach it.
......She stroked it gently, the way you might pet a cat.
``......Do you know about the large storehouse just behind the shrine grounds?@k@vS02/05/130500044. It's called the ritual storehouse.``
...My breath literally stopped.
...But Rika-chan showed not the slightest hint of intimidation.
``......On the night of the festival, a cat apparently snuck into that storehouse.``
Rika-chan knew.
...She could see that I was terrified.
......And I could tell she was choosing her words with desperate care, trying not to frighten me any further.
``......The cat had always wanted to explore inside the storehouse.@k@vS02/05/130500050. ...But I was mean and never let it look inside.``
``......So, on the night of the festival...@k@vS02/05/130500052. ...the cat couldn't hold back any longer, and went in.``
The cat...
...It was clearly meant to be Takano-san.
...No, wait... was it... meant to be me...?
``......Inside, it turned out... there were many things that frightened the cat.``
Rika-chan made an adorable little gesture, like a ghost popping out to scare someone.
``......The cat was absolutely shocked.@k@vS02/05/130500055. ......It bolted away as fast as it could -- rattling, shivering, meow meow -- it was quite the scene.``
``Th-... this cat... who are you saying it is...?``
``......The cat is a cat.@k@vS02/05/130500057. Meow meow.``
Rika-chan steadfastly avoided saying it outright.
......But the cat was, without question... me.
``Rika-chan...@k@vS02/01/130100481. ...Please... tell me.@k@vS02/01/130100482. What should... the cat do now?``
...Maybe it was a reckless question.
...By asking it, I had essentially admitted to being the cat.
``......The cat is just a cat, so all it needs to do is keep meowing and it will be just fine.``
``E-Everything will be fine... you can't just say that so casually...!``
``......Can't it just keep meowing?``
By now... Rika-chan and I had built an entire conversation... using 'cat' as our code word.
I hadn't said it outright... but... I had already admitted everything.
I didn't want to end up like those two.
...I didn't want to become a sacrifice, like Shion had said.
......That fear was why... I had stubbornly kept my mouth shut.@k ...But now... I just couldn't... anymore......
``...Just meowing... isn't enough.@k@vS02/01/130100485. ...A dog saw the cat... sneaking in to cause mischief.``
``...That's right.@k@vS02/01/130100487. ...The dog.@k@vS02/01/130100488. ...It keeps coming to the cat... one after another... demanding to know if it snuck inside.``
For the first time, Rika-chan's expression clouded over.
...She tried to hide it, but I immediately noticed the shadow fall across her eyes.
The silence frightened me... so I urged her on.@k ...Then Rika-chan smiled.
``......It's all right.@k@vS02/05/130500063. I'll protect the cat.``
...Huh?
...Those words were so reassuring... I thought for a moment I must have misheard.
``......The cat is very frightened... but truly, it's not as bad as it thinks.@k@vS02/05/130500065. I believe the dogs are simply making a mistake.``
``Wh-... it's not that bad...?``
``......The cat is worrying too much.@k@vS02/05/130500067. I'll take care of it, I promise.``
She'll take care of it... Rika-chan will...?
But... lost and directionless as I was right now... there had never been a moment when Rika-chan looked more dependable.
``...It'll be a little tough, but I'll do my very best.``
``Fight-o! Yeah!``
She clenched her small fist and thrust it up toward the sky.
...Rika-chan said the cat just needed to keep meowing.
...She told me to leave everything to her.
......W-Was that really... all it would take... to settle this whole thing...?!
``Will you... really be okay handling this... Rika-chan?``
``...If I don't give it my all, something terrible might happen to the dogs too.``
.........I had completely lost track of what Rika-chan was talking about.
She had been speaking under the assumption that I already knew certain things.
...So at the beginning, I could follow along.
...But somewhere in the middle, I stopped understanding entirely.
...Still, one thing was clear...... don't worry. Leave it to me.
Rika-chan stood on tiptoes once more, patted me on the head, and gave me a warm smile.
......I was so deeply moved by that reassurance... that despite myself... tears nearly spilled from my eyes...
``...I'm... sorry...@k@vS02/01/130100493. ...The cat... it really was... just a little bit of mischief.@k@vS02/01/130100494. ...It never imagined... things would come to this......``
``......The cat is far too much of a scaredy-cat.@k@vS02/05/130500072. ...That's exactly why I didn't want it to see what was inside.``
...Rika-chan was absolutely right.
changeReason: Character name 'Rika' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: ......It was far too disturbing for someone driven by mere curiosity...
...That was precisely why they had kept it locked away where no one could easily look...
``Rika-chan.@k@vS02/01/130100496. ......What will happen... to the cat?@k@vS02/01/130100497. ...Two of the cats that snuck in... they... that night, they......``
I choked on my words.
......I had heard that Tomitake-san and Takano-san's deaths were being kept secret.
...There was no guarantee Rika-chan knew about them.
``............Tomitake and Takano?``
The sudden use of real names made my body lurch.@k ...Though at this point, that reaction was a bit late.
``......You should forget about them.``
A chill........shot down my spine... freezing it again.

``......Yes.@k@vS02/05/130500076. Remembering those two won't do you any good, Keiichi.@k@vS02/05/130500077. ......The more you think about it, the more frightened you'll become.@k@vS02/05/130500078. So it's better to forget as quickly as you can.``
She said something utterly terrifying... without letting her smile waver even slightly.
``......I don't know where you heard about it, but you should scrub it from your mind completely -- like washing yourself clean in the bath.``
``F-Forget?! ...Rika-chan... you know, don't you?!@k@vS02/01/130100500. You know... how those two died......!!!``
``......However they died, it has nothing to do with you, Keiichi.``
She said it with absolute finality.
changeReason: Character name 'Rika' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: ...Nothing to do with me.
...So forget.
......The girl before me -- innocent, earnest... someone I had considered dependable just moments ago -- her shadow... suddenly deepened.
......Don't get involved with their deaths.@k ......But I'll help you, Keiichi.
Those two contradictory statements... warped and twisted my image of the girl standing before me.
``...Wh-What about... Shion?@k@vS02/01/130100502. ...What's going to... happen to her......?``
Bringing up Shion's name might have been dangerous.
...But I couldn't stop myself from asking.
...Rika-chan saw through everything anyway.@k ...There was no point trying to hide anything from her now...
``......The younger of the sister cats?@k@vS02/05/130500082. Meow meow.``
``Y-Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100504. ...The little sister cat.``
Rika-chan seemed to consider this for a moment.
...Even that brief silence was unbearable for me...
``......The big sister cat is angry.@k@vS02/05/130500084. The little sister cat did something bad, so she's very angry.``
``The big sister cat......``
......That had to mean Mion...@k ...I recalled being interrogated by her just yesterday.
``......Keiichi.@k@vS02/05/130500086. ...The big sister cat is in a very foul mood.@k@vS02/05/130500087. ...For a while, I think it's best to leave her be.``
A very foul mood... those words sent a chill through me...
...Mion... was angry.
She was angry... that we had violated the ritual storehouse...
``......Let's cancel club activities starting today.``
``......Because she needs to be left alone.``
Rika-chan spoke with a finality that left no room for argument.
...Listen here, Keiichi Maebara.
...Don't go against Rika-chan right now.
......She's the only person... who said she would help you...
Just as she said -- leave it to her.
......She told me... the cat just needs to keep meowing...
...I'll forget everything, just like she said.@k I'll leave it all to her.
......I'll forget it all -- Takano-san, Tomitake-san, everything I did that night...!
Just then, the distant sound of a bell signaling the end of lunch break reached our ears.
``W-Well then... let's head back to class...``
As if seeing through my cowardice -- ready to dump everything on her and flee -- Rika-chan called out to stop me.
``.........If the misguided dog tries to bite the cat, please let me know.``
The misguided dog...
...She had put it in such an adorable way... and yet the true meaning lurking beneath those words... was deeply unsettling.
``......Who is... the misguided dog?``

``......The dog that bit the village chief.@k@vS02/05/130500093. ......I don't know why it bit the village chief.@k@vS02/05/130500094. If it was going to bite anyone, the mischievous cat should have come first.``
...Every drop of blood in my body... felt as though it froze solid in an instant.
......Rika-chan had acknowledged it.
...She had admitted that if the next victims of this terrible calamity were to appear... it would be Shion or me.
I could not fathom... the true nature of the girl standing before me...
......My suspicion gave way to fear... which in turn threatened to transform into raw anger...
Was she... my savior who would protect me...@k ...or some unfathomable being... trying to ensnare me in a horrific tradition and have me killed?@k ......I couldn't tell......
``......I'm sorry.@k@vS02/05/130500096. ......The cat is already so scared... and I went and said something to frighten it even more.@k@vS02/05/130500097. ...I'm truly sorry.``
Rika-chan bowed her head -- hastily, almost as an afterthought.
...But... she was right. I had completely shut down.@k ...I could feel my body trembling uncontrollably.
So when Rika-chan reached out to pat my head... I instinctively recoiled...
Rika-chan's face filled with profound sadness... but I simply could not bring myself to offer my head to her again...
Later that day, Rika-chan said she wasn't in the mood and proposed we skip club activities.
When someone isn't feeling up to it, they have the right to veto.@k ...Rena and the others looked terribly disappointed...
...The big sister cat is in a very foul mood.
Remembering Rika-chan's words... I stole a quiet glance at Mion's expression.
But if I could just forget how she had grilled me yesterday, her behavior seemed no different from usual...
